title: Reverse String
constraints: s[i] is a printable ASCII character.
followUps: Solve it in-place with two pointers in O(n) time and O(1) extra space.
Solve it recursively by swapping s[left] with s[right] and recursing on left+1, right-1. What is the stack space?
Compare with the approach `s.slice().reverse()`. Why is that not allowed by the in-place requirement?
